title: ROCInsight AI Analysis Output
description: JSON output schema for 'rocinsight analyze --format json' and the rocinsight.ai_analysis Python API. Consumers MUST check schema_version before parsing.

schema_version.description: Version of this JSON schema. Tools MUST validate this field before parsing. Breaking changes increment the major number. See SCHEMA_CHANGELOG.md for migration guidance.

metadata.description: Metadata about the analysis run itself.

rocpd_version.description: Version of the rocprofiler-sdk / rocpd package that produced this output.

analysis_version.description: Version of the AI analysis module. Follows SemVer.

database_file.description: Absolute or relative path to the rocpd .db file that was analyzed. Null for Tier 0 (source-only) analysis where no database is provided.

analysis_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the analysis was performed.

analysis_duration_ms.description: Wall-clock time taken to run the analysis in milliseconds.

custom_prompt.description: User-supplied question or focus area passed via --prompt. May be omitted or null when not provided.

profiling_info.description: Information about the profiling session that produced the database.

total_duration_ns.description: Wall-clock duration of the profiled application in nanoseconds.

profiling_mode.description: How the profiling data was collected. 'source_only' is used for Tier 0 (static source analysis with no database). Determines which analysis tiers are available.

analysis_tier.description: Highest analysis tier available. 0=source-only (no database), 1=trace only, 2=hardware counters, 3=PC sampling, 4=thread trace.

gpus.description: List of GPU devices present in the profiling session.

agent_id.description: HSA agent ID for this GPU.

summary.description: High-level performance summary and bottleneck classification.

overall_assessment.description: Human-readable one-to-three sentence summary of the workload performance.

primary_bottleneck.description: Classified primary bottleneck type for the workload.

confidence.description: Confidence score for the bottleneck classification (0.0 to 1.0). Higher values indicate stronger evidence.

key_findings.description: Ordered list of the most significant findings from the analysis. First item is most important.

execution_breakdown.description: Time distribution across execution categories. Null for Tier 0 (source-only) analysis where no database is available. All *_ns values are in nanoseconds, all *_pct values sum to approximately 100.0.

total_runtime_ns.description: Total observed runtime in nanoseconds (max_end - min_start across all operations).

kernel_time_ns.description: Total GPU kernel execution time in nanoseconds.

kernel_time_pct.description: Percentage of total runtime spent in GPU kernels.

memcpy_time_ns.description: Total memory copy time in nanoseconds (all directions: H2D, D2H, D2D).

memcpy_time_pct.description: Percentage of total runtime spent in memory copies.

api_overhead_ns.description: Estimated API and launch overhead in nanoseconds.

api_overhead_pct.description: Percentage of total runtime spent in API and launch overhead.

idle_time_ns.description: GPU idle time in nanoseconds (gaps between operations).

idle_time_pct.description: Percentage of total runtime the GPU was idle.

hotspots.description: Top kernels ranked by total execution time (descending). Contains up to --top-kernels entries.

rank.description: 1-based rank by total execution time (1 = hottest kernel).

name.description: Demangled kernel name as reported by rocprofv3.

calls.description: Total number of dispatches (invocations) of this kernel.

total_duration_ns.description: Sum of all dispatch durations for this kernel in nanoseconds.

pct_of_total.description: Percentage of total runtime this kernel accounts for.

memory_analysis.description: Memory copy statistics grouped by transfer direction. Keys are direction strings.

count.description: Number of memory copy operations in this direction.

total_bytes.description: Total bytes transferred in this direction.

total_duration_ns.description: Total time spent on copies in this direction, in nanoseconds.

avg_duration_ns.description: Average duration per copy operation in nanoseconds.

bandwidth_gbps.description: Achieved memory bandwidth in gigabytes per second (GB/s). Computed as total_bytes / total_duration_ns, which has units of bytes/ns and is numerically equal to GB/s. Note: the field name suffix '_gbps' is an abbreviation for 'gigabytes per second', not gigabits per second (Gbps).

hardware_counters.description: Tier 2 hardware performance counter data. Present in all outputs; check has_counters before using counter fields.

has_counters.description: True when the database contains hardware counter data (collected via --pmc). When false, all other fields in this object are null or empty.

metrics.description: Derived metrics computed from raw counters. Null when has_counters is false.

gpu_utilization_pct.description: GPU utilization percentage derived from GRBM_GUI_ACTIVE / GRBM_COUNT * 100.

avg_waves.description: Average wave occupancy (active wavefronts per CU) from SQ_WAVES counter.

counters.description: Raw hardware counter statistics keyed by counter name (e.g., GRBM_COUNT, SQ_WAVES). Null when has_counters is false.

sample_count.description: Number of samples collected for this counter.

avg_value.description: Average counter value across all samples.

recommendations.description: Prioritized, actionable optimization recommendations with structured tool commands. Ordered by priority (HIGH first) then by estimated impact.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings about missing data, limited analysis scope, or degraded accuracy.

severity.description: 'warning' means the analysis may be incomplete. 'info' is purely informational.

recommendation.description: Suggested action to resolve or work around this warning.

errors.description: Non-fatal errors encountered during analysis. An empty array means the analysis completed successfully. If this array is non-empty, some analysis sections may be incomplete.

llm_enhanced_explanation.description: Natural language explanation generated by an LLM (Anthropic Claude or OpenAI GPT) when --llm is enabled. Null when LLM enhancement was not requested or failed. Tools MUST treat this as supplementary; all actionable data is in the structured fields above.

tier0.description: Tier 0 static source-code analysis results. Present when --source-dir is used (source_only mode) or in combined mode (-i + --source-dir). Absent in pure Tier 1/2/3/4 runs.

source_dir.description: Path to the source directory that was scanned.

analysis_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the source analysis.

programming_model.description: Detected GPU programming model (e.g. HIP, CUDA, OpenCL, Python/PyTorch).

kernel_count.description: Number of GPU kernel definitions detected.

detected_kernels.description: List of detected GPU kernel definitions.

detected_patterns.description: List of GPU usage patterns detected in the source.

risk_areas.description: List of potential performance risk areas identified.

already_instrumented.description: True when ROCTx markers or other instrumentation are already present.

roctx_marker_count.description: Number of ROCTx marker calls found in the source.

recommendations.description: Ordered profiling and optimization recommendations from static analysis.

suggested_counters.description: List of hardware counters recommended for the next profiling pass.

suggested_first_command.description: Ready-to-run rocprofv3 command suggested as the first profiling step.

llm_explanation.description: LLM-generated explanation of the source analysis findings.

interval_timeline.description: GPU timeline computed via interval arithmetic (TraceLens methodology)

kernel_categories.description: Kernel execution time aggregated by TraceLens op category

att_trace.description: Tier 3 Advanced Thread Trace instruction-level stall analysis. Present when --att-dir is used and ATT CSV data is found. Absent in Tier 1/2 runs.

has_att_data.description: True when valid ATT CSV data was parsed successfully.

reason.description: Error or status message when has_att_data is false.

summary.description: Aggregated ATT statistics across all traced kernels.

kernel_count.description: Number of kernels with valid ATT trace data.

high_stall_kernels.description: Number of kernels with stall_ratio >= 0.60 on the top stalling instruction.

kernels.description: Per-kernel ATT analysis results, sorted by total_weighted_stall descending.

csv_file.description: Path to the ATT stats CSV file for this kernel.

instruction_count.description: Total number of instructions parsed from the CSV.

avg_stall_ratio.description: Average stall_ratio across all instructions (stall_cycles / total_latency_cycles).

stall_category.description: Classified bottleneck type derived from top stalling instruction PC prefix.

total_weighted_stall.description: Sum of (stall_cycles * hitcount) across all instructions — primary sort key.

top_stalling_instructions.description: Top 10 instructions ranked by weighted_stall (stall_cycles * hitcount) descending.

pc_offset.description: Instruction identifier or PC offset as reported by rocprofv3 ATT.

hitcount.description: Number of times this instruction was sampled.

total_latency_cycles.description: Total latency cycles accumulated for this instruction.

stall_cycles.description: Total stall cycles for this instruction.

stall_ratio.description: Fraction of latency spent stalling (stall_cycles / total_latency_cycles).

weighted_stall.description: stall_cycles * hitcount — composite severity score used for ranking.

source_line.description: Source file and line number associated with this instruction, if available.

recommendation_command.description: A single structured command recommendation for one ROCm profiling tool.

tool.description: The ROCm profiling tool to invoke. 'rocprofv3' for trace/counter collection, 'rocprof-sys' for system-level profiling (Omnitrace), 'rocprof-compute' for kernel-level hardware counter analysis.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this command does and why it is recommended for this issue.

flags.description: Boolean flags to pass to the tool (no associated value). Order is preserved in full_command.

args.description: Named arguments with optional values to pass to the tool. Arguments with null value are treated as boolean flags and merged into 'flags' semantically, but kept separate for structured access.

name.description: Argument name including leading dashes, e.g. '--pmc', '-d', '-o'.

value.description: Argument value. Null means the argument is a flag with no value (duplicate of flags for structured access). String values are included verbatim in full_command.

full_command.description: Complete ready-to-run shell command including tool name, all flags, all args, and application placeholder '-- ./app'. Consumers can display this directly for copy-paste. Application arguments should be appended after '-- ./app'.

recommendation.description: A single optimization recommendation with structured text explanation and tool commands.

id.description: Stable identifier for this recommendation type. Can be used by tools to suppress or track specific recommendations across runs.

priority.description: Recommendation priority. HIGH items should be addressed first. INFO items are observations that do not require action.

category.description: Optimization category for grouping and filtering.

issue.description: Concise description of the detected performance problem, including measured values where available.

suggestion.description: High-level recommendation explaining what to do to address the issue.

actions.description: Concrete, numbered implementation steps to resolve the issue. Ordered from most to least impactful.

estimated_impact.description: Qualitative or quantitative estimate of the expected performance improvement if this recommendation is implemented.
examples: 15-30% reduction in total runtime when transfers dominate
Eliminates ~25% of launch overhead for workloads with many small kernels
May improve GPU utilization from 45% toward >80%

commands.description: Ordered list of structured profiling commands to help investigate or validate this recommendation. Each command targets a specific ROCm tool. Consumers should offer these as copy-paste suggestions.

examples.description: Non-normative examples showing typical field values for each analysis tier.

summary.overall_assessment: Workload is memory-transfer-bound with significant launch overhead from many small kernel dispatches.
key_findings: Memory copies consume 25.3% of total runtime (40 small transfers)
add_scalar_kernel dominates at 45.2% of kernel time
180 kernel dispatches with average duration 8.7 μs (high launch overhead)

recommendations.issue: Memory copies account for 25.3% of execution time across 40 small transfers
recommendations.suggestion: Batch the 40 small transfers into 1-2 large transfers to amortize per-transfer overhead
actions: 1. Allocate a single pinned host buffer for all data
2. Copy all data in one hipMemcpy call
3. Use hipMemcpyAsync with streams to overlap with computation

commands.description: Trace HIP and HSA memory copy operations with timing to measure transfer bottleneck
Detailed memory transfer timeline with PCIe bandwidth and overlap analysis

warnings.message: No hardware counters collected. Analysis limited to Tier 1 (trace data only).
warnings.recommendation: Collect counters with: rocprofv3 --pmc GRBM_COUNT GRBM_GUI_ACTIVE SQ_WAVES -- ./app
